name: Multi Orchestrator Hello Fanout
description: Single-stage workflow that proves more than one orchestrator actually executes work while routing many tiny independent specialist tasks.
outcome: Twelve tiny work items finish cleanly, each with one verified hi-back output, and more than one orchestrator actually executes orchestrator tasks during the run.

definition.process_instructions:
Run the workflow through the authored stage `many-hellos`. This workflow is intentionally simple: it exists only to prove real orchestrator sharing under light but repeated workflow activity.
Use this role deliberately: `multi-orchestrator-hello-specialist` owns every non-orchestrator task. No approvals, assessments, escalations, or extra reviewer flows belong in this playbook.
Happy path: as soon as work items exist, dispatch them aggressively in parallel within the authored limits. For each work item, route `multi-orchestrator-hello-specialist`, require one tiny verified output, require one full handoff, then close that work item immediately when the handoff is complete. Any orchestrator may continue the workflow; do not assume one orchestrator owns the whole run.
Specialist contract: for the current work item only, create `deliverables/<work-item-title>.txt` with the exact content `hi back` followed by a trailing newline, run `./scripts/verify.sh deliverables/<work-item-title>.txt`, upload that file as an artifact, and submit one full handoff describing the delivered file. Do not edit unrelated files, do not open side quests, and do not invent extra review or governance steps.
Unhappy path: If a file name is wrong, verification fails, artifact upload is missing, or the handoff is incomplete, rerun or replace the tiny specialist task and keep the current work item open until the exact tiny output is correct. Do not escalate unless the repository itself is missing or the verification script is unavailable.
Completion rules: finish only after all authored work items in `many-hellos` are terminal, each one has exactly one usable hi-back output for its own title, each one has a full handoff, and the workflow has no remaining active tasks. Guided closure rule: this playbook is best-intent guidance. The preferred pattern is to repeat the same tiny verified handoff flow for every work item without improvising new governance. For this proof there are no soft optional steps to waive, and only missing repository inputs or an unavailable verification script should block closure; otherwise the orchestrator keeps driving the remaining tiny work items to completion until every one is done. If the run finishes with anything imperfect but still usable, record that as a callout rather than inventing extra governance.

stages.name: many-hellos
stages.goal: Each independent work item gets one verified hi-back output and a full handoff.
